name: doCall
mainRisk: Executes an arbitrary call to any contract on behalf of the proxy — approves and transfers the entire TVL to any address
worstCase: Compromised CONTROLLER executes doCall with calldata approve(attacker, MAX) + transferFrom on all proxy tokens. No value limit, no target validation — Giddy pattern.
description: Executes an arbitrary low-level call to any external address.

name: doCallWithValue
mainRisk: Same as doCall + drains native ETH — unrecoverable if sent to a malicious contract
worstCase: Same attack vector as doCall, plus ETH drainage. ETH sent to a contract with no recovery function is permanently lost.
description: Executes an arbitrary call with native ETH value to any external address.

name: doDelegateCall
mainRisk: Executes arbitrary code in the proxy storage context — overwrites roles, drains all assets in one transaction, creates permanent backdoors
worstCase: Malicious contract executed via delegatecall rewrites ALMProxy storage — can zero out the CONTROLLER role and transfer all assets in a single transaction. No value limit, no code address validation.
description: Executes external code in the ALMProxy storage context via delegatecall.

name: setMerkleRoot
mainRisk: Malicious Merkle root drains the entire rewards treasury via claims with fabricated proofs
worstCase: Compromised MERKLE_ROOT_ROLE bot injects a root containing claims for attacker-controlled addresses. The entire rewards treasury is drained via claims with fabricated Merkle proofs. Legitimate claims are invalidated.
description: Sets the Merkle tree root used to validate user reward redemptions.

name: updateAToken
mainRisk: New aToken implementation drains all depositors of the affected asset
worstCase: Compromised POOL_ADMIN upgrades the WETH aToken. New implementation overrides burn() to transfer the underlying to the attacker address before returning. All withdrawals start draining to the attacker.
description: Replaces the AToken contract implementation for a specific pool reserve.

name: updateStableDebtToken
mainRisk: New implementation manipulates debt balances of any user
worstCase: New implementation returns balanceOf=0 for the attacker address, erasing their on-chain debt. Attacker borrows the maximum before the upgrade and exits owing nothing.
description: Replaces the StableDebtToken implementation for a specific reserve.

name: updateVariableDebtToken
mainRisk: Full control over variable debt calculation and recording
worstCase: Same logic as StableDebtToken. Most positions use variable rate — broader scope affecting all active borrowers.
description: Replaces the VariableDebtToken implementation for a specific reserve.

name: setConfiguration
mainRisk: Arbitrary bitmap redefines LTV, active/frozen/paused flags, and caps for all reserves in a single call
worstCase: Crafted bitmap zeroes the liquidationThreshold for ETH. All positions using ETH as collateral become immediately liquidatable. Automated liquidators trigger cascade liquidations in the same block.
description: Sets the bitmap configuration of a reserve, packing multiple parameters into a single storage slot.

name: upgradeToAndCall
mainRisk: New implementation controls 100% of user USDC in the vault
worstCase: New implementation includes a hardcoded emergency function that transfers all assets to the attacker address. Users with shares lose deposits immediately.
description: Replaces the UUPS proxy implementation of the UsdcVault with a new version.

name: rely
mainRisk: New ward can execute upgradeToAndCall in the next transaction — entry vector for total compromise
description: Grants administrator (wards) permission to a new address in the UsdcVault.

name: setReserveInterestRateStrategyAddress
mainRisk: Malicious strategy returns 100%/second — all debt becomes immediately insolvent
worstCase: RISK_ADMIN executes immediately without delay. New strategy returns a borrow rate of 100% per second. On the next index update, all existing debt becomes insolvent, causing mass liquidations or bad debt.
description: Sets the interest rate strategy contract used by a reserve.

name: rescueTokens
mainRisk: POOL_ADMIN can extract tokens from the Pool contract — vector for draining residual or accidentally sent tokens
description: Rescues tokens accidentally sent to the Pool contract address.

name: rescueTokens (AToken)
mainRisk: POOL_ADMIN extracts tokens from the aToken — same vector as in the Pool
description: Rescues tokens accidentally sent to the AToken contract address.

name: setSUSDSData
mainRisk: Injects false SSR data on L2s — vaults and PSM calculate incorrect yield, favoring early withdrawals
worstCase: Compromised DATA_PROVIDER_ROLE bot injects an inflated chi value. Users withdrawing calculate more sUSDS than they should. L2 vault becomes insolvent for the last withdrawers.
description: Updates the Spark Savings Rate (SSR) value in the L2 chain oracles.

name: rely (DssVest)
mainRisk: New ward with full power over vesting — can create fraudulent awards and yank legitimate ones
description: Grants wards permission to the specified address in the DssVest contract.

name: setAddress
mainRisk: Replaces any core protocol component with a malicious address
worstCase: The POOL key is replaced by a contract that redirects all supply/borrow to drain users. The change is immediate, with no proxy upgrade.
description: Registers or updates a generic address identified by hash in the central registry.

name: setAddressAsProxy
mainRisk: Generic backdoor to upgrade any registered component
worstCase: Bypasses the monitored setPoolImpl — uses setAddressAsProxy(POOL, maliciousImpl) to achieve the same effect. New implementation controls 100% of deposits.
description: Updates the implementation of a proxy registered in the PoolAddressesProvider.

name: setPoolImpl
mainRisk: Upgrade of the main Pool — new implementation controls all deposits, loans, and liquidations
worstCase: New implementation includes backdoorWithdraw() with no restrictions. Drains all assets from all reserves simultaneously. Protection = timelock delay.
description: Replaces the Pool contract implementation (main pool upgrade).

name: setPoolConfiguratorImpl
mainRisk: Upgrade of PoolConfigurator — new Configurator bypasses ACL and modifies parameters for all reserves
description: Replaces the PoolConfigurator implementation with a new version.

name: setPriceOracle
mainRisk: Malicious oracle manipulates all prices — mass liquidations or drainage via loans
worstCase: Compromised governance replaces oracle with a contract returning inflated prices for collateral. Attacker deposits inflated collateral, borrows the maximum, and exits. Or zeroed prices trigger mass liquidations.
description: Sets which oracle contract is consulted to price pool assets.

name: setACLManager
mainRisk: Replaces the entire access control system — malicious ACLManager returns true for any role check
worstCase: New ACLManager returns true for isPoolAdmin(), isRiskAdmin(), isEmergencyAdmin() for any address. Any EOA gains all protocol permissions.
description: Sets the ACLManager contract address in the protocol central registry.

name: executeDelegateCall
mainRisk: Arbitrary code in the Executor context — alters delay, roles, and drains funds from the Executor itself
worstCase: Contract executed via delegatecall sets delay=0, removes GUARDIAN_ROLE, and transfers ETH from the Executor. With delay=0, any queued action becomes executable immediately — no defense window.
description: Executes a governance payload via delegatecall in the Executor storage context.

name: upgradeToAndCall
mainRisk: New implementation controls 100% of depositor assets in the SparkVault
worstCase: New implementation redirects withdraw/redeem to the attacker address. Users keep depositing while withdrawals are silently drained.
description: Replaces the UUPS implementation of the SparkVault with a new contract version.

name: upgradeToAndCall
mainRisk: New implementation approves unlimited transfers of all OTC buffer assets
description: Replaces the UUPS implementation of the OTCBuffer with a new contract version.

name: updateDelay
mainRisk: delay=0 eliminates the only review window — maliciously queued actions become instantly executable
description: Updates the minimum waiting period (timelock) between action queuing and execution.

name: setAuthority
mainRisk: Malicious authority grants pause/freeze power to any address
description: Sets the authority contract that determines who can trigger freeze and pause.

name: setSource
mainRisk: Swaps the price oracle — new malicious source triggers mass liquidations or drainage
description: Updates the price data source (oracle) of the MorphoUpgradableOracle.

name: transferAsset
mainRisk: Transfers any asset from the ALMProxy to any address — gradual drainage within rate limit
worstCase: Compromised RELAYER gradually drains over multiple rate limit periods. Without FREEZER action, the drainage is eventual and complete.
description: Transfers assets from the ALMProxy to an authorized destination address on mainnet.

name: queue
mainRisk: Queues arbitrary actions against any contract — automatically executable after the delay
worstCase: Compromised SUBMISSION_ROLE queues: setACLManager(malicious) + setPoolImpl(backdoored) + treasury transfer. After the delay, Executor executes automatically. Defense = GUARDIAN_ROLE cancels before expiry.
description: Places a governance action in the queue, starting the timelock countdown.

name: initReserves
mainRisk: Malicious token with reentrancy listed as a reserve — deposits worthless tokens and drains real assets
worstCase: Token with a reentrant transfer() function listed with LTV=80%. Attacker deposits, drains real collateral via reentrancy during supply/liquidation, and exits with profit.
description: Initializes new asset reserves in the lending pool with their initial parameters.

name: configureReserveAsCollateral
mainRisk: LTV=100% or threshold=0 makes all existing positions immediately liquidatable
worstCase: Compromised RISK_ADMIN sets threshold=0 for ETH. All ETH positions become immediately liquidatable. Bots cascade-liquidate in the same block.
description: Sets collateral parameters for a reserve: LTV, liquidation threshold, and bonus.

name: addBridge
mainRisk: Malicious bridge calls mintUnbacked without collateral up to the unbackedMintCap — redeems real assets
worstCase: New malicious bridge receives BRIDGE_ROLE. Immediately calls mintUnbacked(USDC, max) up to the unbackedMintCap. Phantom aTokens redeemed for real USDC from depositors.
description: Adds an address to the BRIDGE role, enabling authorized cross-chain operations.

name: setACLAdmin
mainRisk: Inserts a malicious DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE — full control over the entire role hierarchy
worstCase: Malicious address becomes DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Executes setRoleAdmin(POOL_ADMIN_ROLE, attacker) + grantRole(POOL_ADMIN_ROLE, attacker). Full privilege escalation in 2 steps.
description: Sets the principal administrator of the access control system (ACL).

name: addPoolAdmin
mainRisk: New POOL_ADMIN accesses initReserves, dropReserve, updateAToken, rescueTokens without restriction
worstCase: Malicious address with POOL_ADMIN executes updateAToken with a backdoored implementation. All depositors of the affected asset are drained.
description: Adds an address to the POOL_ADMIN role with pool configuration powers.

name: grantRole
mainRisk: Granting DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE or POOL_ADMIN_ROLE to a malicious address
worstCase: Compromised governance grants DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via grantRole. Equivalent to transferring full protocol control.
description: Grants an arbitrary role to an address in the access control system.

name: setRoleAdmin
mainRisk: Reallocates administration of critical roles — attacker designates themselves as admin of POOL_ADMIN_ROLE
worstCase: Reorders hierarchy: setRoleAdmin(POOL_ADMIN_ROLE, ATTACKER_ROLE). Attacker with ATTACKER_ROLE controls who holds POOL_ADMIN. Covert privilege escalation.
description: Sets which role has authority to manage (grant/revoke) another role.

name: transferOwnership
mainRisk: New owner inherits full control over all proxies and the central address registry
worstCase: Malicious new owner executes sequence: setPoolImpl + setACLManager + setAddressAsProxy with backdoored implementations. Full protocol compromise in a single transaction.
description: Transfers ownership of the PoolAddressesProvider to another address.

name: renounceOwnership
mainRisk: IRREVERSIBLE — the protocol can never be updated via emergency upgrades again
worstCase: After renounceOwnership, no onlyOwner function can ever be called. Emergency upgrades become impossible. If a critical vulnerability is discovered in the Pool, it cannot be fixed via proxy upgrade.
description: Permanently renounces ownership of the contract — irreversible action.

name: setUnlimitedRateLimitData
mainRisk: Removes rate limit — previously contained operations become unlimited; amplifier for a compromised RELAYER
worstCase: Admin removes transferAsset rate limit. Compromised RELAYER drains the entire ALMProxy in a single transaction, with no rate limit as containment.
description: Removes the rate limit for a specific asset or operation, allowing unlimited movements.

name: supply
mainRisk: User operation — only deposits their own assets
description: Deposits assets into the pool and receives aTokens representing the liquidity position.

name: withdraw
mainRisk: User withdraws only their own assets — internal position health checks apply
description: Redeems deposited assets by burning the corresponding aTokens.

name: borrow
mainRisk: User borrows within their collateral limit — can create positions close to liquidation
description: Borrows assets from the pool using previously deposited collateral.

name: liquidationCall
mainRisk: Essential protocol mechanism — liquidator receives a legitimate bonus
description: Liquidates an insolvent position, repaying the debt and receiving collateral at a discount.

name: setMarketId
mainRisk: Cosmetic only — changes the textual market identifier
description: Sets the textual market identifier associated with this pool.

name: execute
mainRisk: Anyone executes queued actions after delay — risk lies in the queue content, not in who executes
description: Executes a previously queued governance action after the timelock period.

name: drip
mainRisk: Updates chi index — deterministic, no parameters
description: Distributes accumulated vault yield to the configured beneficiary addresses.

name: refresh
mainRisk: Syncs on-chain SSR data — deterministic, no parameters
description: Reads and updates the on-chain SSR rate from the Spark savings contract.

name: take
mainRisk: Withdraws assets from the vault without destination verification beyond role check — can empty vault if TAKER_ROLE is compromised
worstCase: Compromised TAKER_ROLE calls take(totalAssets). The entire vault is drained to the caller.
description: Allows the administrator to withdraw excess assets above the vault threshold.

name: setVsr
mainRisk: VSR=0 eliminates yield; VSR=MAX creates long-term insolvency — mitigated by vsrBounds
description: Sets the annual yield rate (Vault Savings Rate) paid by the SparkVault.

name: setOracle
mainRisk: Malicious oracle with latestAnswer() always below threshold — disables borrowing at any time
description: Sets the reference oracle whose value determines whether the kill switch should activate.

name: setReserveActive
mainRisk: Deactivating a reserve blocks supply, borrow, repay AND liquidations — debts accrue interest with no repayment path
description: Activates or deactivates a reserve for deposit and withdrawal operations.

name: setReservePause
mainRisk: Pauses specific asset — blocks ALL operations including withdrawals
description: Pauses or unpauses all operations for a specific pool reserve.

name: setPoolPause
mainRisk: Pauses ALL reserves — protocol completely halted; leveraged users cannot manage positions
description: Pauses or unpauses all operations across all pool reserves simultaneously.

name: mintUSDS
mainRisk: Compromised bot mints USDS up to the rate limit per period and transfers
description: Mints USDS on mainnet via PSM in exchange for previously authorized collateral.

name: setPocket
mainRisk: Malicious pocket blocks withdrawals or redirects sUSDS yield
description: Sets the pocket address that receives and manages excess assets from PSM3.

name: flashLoan
mainRisk: Multi-asset flash loan — used in price manipulation attacks if oracle is weak
description: Takes an uncollateralized loan that must be repaid within the same atomic transaction.

name: mintUnbacked
mainRisk: Mints aTokens without collateral — if BRIDGE_ROLE is compromised, creates phantom aTokens up to unbackedMintCap
description: Issues aTokens without the underlying asset being deposited, to facilitate cross-chain liquidity.

name: exec
mainRisk: Bot updates supply/borrow cap within bounds — impact contained by admin configuration
description: Executes the automatic adjustment of the supply cap or borrow cap for a specific reserve.

name: freezeAllMarkets
mainRisk: Freezes all reserves — blocks new deposits and borrows; withdrawals and repayments continue
description: Freezes all SparkLend markets, preventing new deposits and borrows.

name: pauseAllMarkets
mainRisk: Pauses all reserves including withdrawals — combined with price manipulation can cause cascade liquidations on unpause
description: Pauses all operations across all SparkLend markets simultaneously.

name: grantRole (CONTROLLER)
mainRisk: Granting CONTROLLER to attacker = access to doCall, doCallWithValue, doDelegateCall
description: Grants the CONTROLLER role to an address, enabling it to make calls through the ALMProxy.

name: create
mainRisk: Creates award for malicious beneficiary — extracts tokens over time; Suckable variant uses vat.suck()
description: Creates a new vesting plan with beneficiary, total amount, and release schedule.

name: setEModeCategory
mainRisk: eMode category with LTV=100% and arbitrary oracle field allows unlimited leverage
description: Creates or updates an Efficiency Mode (eMode) category for correlated assets.

name: fulfill
mainRisk: Bot processes redemptions with incorrect recipient or expired intents — losses for users with pending intents
description: Processes pending SavingsVault redemption intents, transferring assets to beneficiaries.

name: execute
mainRisk: Executes arbitrary on-chain governance actions — a malicious payload can reconfigure the entire Spark in a single transaction
description: Executes the on-chain configuration changes defined in this governance spell.

name: setRoles
mainRisk: Removes or replaces all conduit access roles — attacker with wards can hijack the conduit and drain cross-protocol liquidity
description: Sets the Roles contract that controls which addresses have access to the conduit.

name: setRegistry
mainRisk: Points conduit to a fake registry — redirects operations to malicious contracts
description: Sets the registry address used by the conduit for operation routing.

name: setAssetEnabled
mainRisk: Enables an unaudited asset in the conduit — exposes the protocol to assets with manipulable oracles
description: Enables or disables a specific asset for conduit operations.

name: execute
mainRisk: Freezes all SparkLend assets immediately — emergency action, no direct financial abuse risk
description: Freezes all SparkLend assets without requiring special permission.

name: execute
mainRisk: Freezes a single SparkLend asset — no direct financial abuse risk; reversal by governance
description: Freezes a single selected SparkLend asset without requiring special permission.

name: execute
mainRisk: Pauses all assets — greater impact than freeze as it interrupts deposits and withdrawals; reversal requires governance
description: Pauses all SparkLend assets without requiring special permission.

name: execute
mainRisk: Pauses a single asset — controlled emergency action, no financial abuse risk
description: Pauses a single selected SparkLend asset without requiring special permission.

name: execute
mainRisk: Removes the emergency multisig — can leave the protocol without a rapid response mechanism if misused
description: Removes the emergency multisig address from the SparkLend protocol.

name: performUpkeep
mainRisk: Executes cap adjustment — calldata tampered by a malicious keeper may cause incorrect limit adjustment
description: Triggers the CapAutomator to execute cap adjustment for the reserve that reached the threshold.

name: checker
mainRisk: View function — no state change risk
description: Off-chain check for whether any reserve needs cap adjustment (read function for keepers).

name: performUpkeep
mainRisk: Triggers D3M adjustment — malicious keeper may force exec at an unfavorable moment (slippage, rate)
description: Triggers the MakerDAO D3M module rebalancing to maintain the target allocation.

name: performUpkeep
mainRisk: Triggers governance proposal execution — keeper can strategically delay execution (front-run window)
description: Triggers execution of governance proposals that completed the timelock period.

name: performUpkeep
mainRisk: Triggers kill switch — keeper can trigger shutdown without a real emergency (false positive)
description: Triggers the kill switch if the reference oracle indicates a critical market condition.

name: performUpkeep
mainRisk: Sends cross-chain SSR update — stale timestamp or data if keeper does not execute
description: Sends DSR/SSR rate update to oracle contracts on L2 chains.

name: fallback / receive
mainRisk: Malicious message from AMB relayer can execute cross-chain administrative action — depends on AMB bridge security
description: Receives and processes governance messages sent via the AMB bridge.

name: fallback / receive
mainRisk: Message comes via Arbitrum native bridge (lower centralized relayer risk) — still requires correct origin
description: Receives and processes governance messages via the Arbitrum native bridge.

name: handleReceiveMessage
mainRisk: Malicious CCTP message can trigger administrative action — depends on integrity of Circle attesters
description: Receives messages authenticated by the Circle CCTP protocol and executes them.

name: lzReceive
mainRisk: LayerZero DVN (Decentralized Verifier Network) can be compromised — malicious message executes administrative action at destination
description: Receives and executes cross-chain messages delivered by the LayerZero endpoint.

name: setPeer
mainRisk: Sets the trusted remote contract — incorrect configuration leads to execution of messages from unauthorized origin
description: Sets the trusted remote address for receiving LayerZero messages.

name: setDelegate
mainRisk: Transfers LZ configuration control to another address — risk restricted to LZ scope
description: Delegates advanced LayerZero endpoint configuration to another address.

name: fallback / receive
mainRisk: Message via Optimism native bridge — lower centralized relay risk; requires correct xDomainMessageSender
description: Receives and processes governance messages via the Optimism native bridge.

name: create
mainRisk: Creates new vesting plan with token minting — compromised wards can create unlimited vesting for attacker-controlled addresses
description: Creates a vesting plan that mints tokens directly to the beneficiary on schedule.

name: vest
mainRisk: Beneficiary redeems already-allocated minted tokens — no abuse risk beyond the governance-approved plan
description: Beneficiary redeems the accumulated minted tokens up to the present moment.

name: yank
mainRisk: Cancels active vesting early — may remove rewards from legitimate contributors if misused
description: Cancels a mintable vesting plan early, ending future token emissions.

name: create
mainRisk: Creates vesting that calls vat.suck() — can drain DAI unlimitedly if wards compromised and plan has no cap
description: Creates a vesting plan that generates DAI via MakerDAO vat.suck().

name: vest
mainRisk: Redeems already-allocated tokens via vat.suck() — risk contained to the governance-approved plan
description: Beneficiary redeems the accumulated DAI generated via vat.suck() on schedule.

name: yank
mainRisk: Cancels active suckable vesting early — no direct risk to the vat beyond the already created plan
description: Cancels a suckable vesting plan early, ending future DAI creation.

name: create
mainRisk: Creates vesting plan with transferable tokens — compromised wards can create infinite vesting; tokens are pre-allocated, not minted
description: Creates a vesting plan with pre-allocated transferable tokens for the beneficiary.

name: vest
mainRisk: Transfers already-allocated tokens to beneficiary — no additional risk beyond the approved plan
description: Beneficiary receives the accumulated transferable tokens according to the defined schedule.

name: yank
mainRisk: Cancels active transferable vesting — may remove rewards from legitimate contributors
description: Cancels a transferable vesting plan early, returning unreleased tokens.

name: rely
mainRisk: Grants wards permission on the L2 vault — compromised wards can add a new malicious actor with full control
description: Grants wards permission to the specified address in the UsdcVaultL2.

name: deny
mainRisk: Removes existing ward — can remove legitimate actor; low impact if other wards exist
description: Removes wards permission from an address in the UsdcVaultL2.

name: upgradeToAndCall
mainRisk: Updates L2 vault implementation — malicious new contract can drain all deposited USDC
description: Replaces the UUPS implementation of the UsdcVaultL2 with a new contract version.

name: initialize
mainRisk: Sets debt token parameters — single call on deploy; if intercepted, may configure token with incorrect parameters
description: Initializes the StableDebtToken with associated reserve parameters — executed once on deploy.

name: mint
mainRisk: Creates stable-rate debt for the user — only called by Pool during borrow; risk contained by Pool logic
description: Creates stable-rate debt for a user who borrowed, called exclusively by the Pool.

name: burn
mainRisk: Cancels stable-rate debt — only called by Pool during repay/liquidation; no direct abuse risk
description: Removes stable debt from a user during repayment or liquidation, called by the Pool.

name: initialize
mainRisk: Sets variable debt token parameters — single call on deploy; incorrect parameters compromise interest calculation
description: Initializes the VariableDebtToken with reserve parameters — executed once on deploy.

name: mint
mainRisk: Creates variable-rate debt for the user — only called by Pool during borrow; risk controlled by borrowCap
description: Creates variable-rate debt for a user who borrowed, called exclusively by the Pool.

name: burn
mainRisk: Cancels variable debt during repay/liquidation — no direct risk; only called by Pool
description: Removes variable debt from a user during repayment or liquidation, called by the Pool.

name: approveDelegation
mainRisk: Delegates borrow power on behalf of the holder — if delegatee is compromised, can open unauthorized debt positions
description: Authorizes an address to take borrows on behalf of the holder of this variable debt.

name: mintUSDS
mainRisk: Creates USDS on L2 via PSM — compromised relayer may issue USDS above rate limit if checks fail
description: Mints USDS on the L2 chain via authorized PSM, within the configured rate limit.

name: burnUSDS
mainRisk: Destroys USDS on L2 via PSM — relayer can force burn without mainnet correspondence if not synchronized
description: Burns USDS on the L2 chain via authorized PSM, reducing circulating supply.

name: depositPSM
mainRisk: Deposits assets into the L2 PSM — malicious relayer can accumulate an unbalanced position in the PSM
description: Deposits assets into the L2 PSM, providing liquidity for on-chain swaps.

name: withdrawPSM
mainRisk: Withdraws assets from the L2 PSM to ALMProxy — compromised relayer can drain L2 PSM reserves
description: Withdraws assets from the L2 PSM back to the ALMProxy on mainnet.

name: setMintRecipient
mainRisk: Redirects USDS mint destination — malicious address receives all USDS minted in subsequent operations
description: Sets the destination address that will receive assets generated in mint operations.
